items.headline: Tech Sell-off Weighs on Broader Market, Healthcare Shines Amid Earnings Reports
items.summary: U.S. equities are experiencing a mixed session today, with a continued sell-off in technology and semiconductor stocks dragging down the Nasdaq, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average shows resilience, buoyed by strong performances in the healthcare and financial sectors. Overall market sentiment remains cautious amid ongoing geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran, which continue to influence oil prices. Investors are closely monitoring new economic data and key earnings reports.

items.statContext: Down 0.37% today, after closing higher on Wednesday.
Slipped 0.76% today, extending tech sector declines.
Edged up 0.14%, supported by defensive and financial stocks.

items.headline: Abbott Laboratories
items.summary: Surged after beating second-quarter earnings expectations and raising its full-year forecast.

items.headline: UnitedHealth Group
items.summary: Gained significantly following strong second-quarter 2026 earnings and an updated full-year outlook.

items.headline: ManpowerGroup
items.summary: Climbed in premarket trading after reporting better-than-expected second-quarter earnings.

items.headline: SanDisk
items.summary: Slipped amid a broader sell-off in semiconductor and memory stocks.

items.headline: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company
items.summary: Fell despite beating earnings, as concerns over heavy capital spending and broader sector weakness weighed on sentiment.

items.headline: SK Hynix
items.summary: Plunged as the semiconductor sell-off continued for a fourth consecutive session.

items.headline: Tech Sector Faces Continued Headwinds, Healthcare Leads Gains
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly chipmakers, is experiencing sustained selling pressure, contributing to the Nasdaq's decline. Conversely, the healthcare sector is demonstrating notable strength, buoyed by positive earnings reports from major companies like Abbott Laboratories and UnitedHealth Group.

items.headline: Retail Sales and Jobless Claims Released, Mortgage Rates Hit One-Year High
items.summary: The Labor Department reported that seasonally adjusted initial jobless claims totaled 208,000 for the week ending July 11, down 8,000 from the prior week. Meanwhile, June retail sales rose 0.2%, with online sales climbing 1.9%. Amid these releases, U.S. mortgage rates reached their highest point in almost a year, with the 30-year rate at 6.55%, reflecting uncertainty regarding the Federal Reserve's next policy moves.

items.headline: UnitedHealth and Abbott Report Strong Q2 Earnings, Netflix Awaits Close
items.summary: UnitedHealth Group and Abbott Laboratories both reported robust second-quarter 2026 earnings, leading to significant stock gains. Netflix is scheduled to release its quarterly results after the market closes today, with investors keenly awaiting insights into its performance. GE Aerospace also has earnings scheduled for today.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Keep Oil Prices Elevated Amid US-Iran Strikes
items.summary: Oil prices are experiencing minor fluctuations but remain at elevated levels as geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran continue. The ongoing conflict and related military strikes are contributing to market uncertainty and underpinning energy prices.

items.headline: TSMC Announces Additional $100 Billion US Investment
items.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) announced an incremental $100 billion investment for advanced semiconductor manufacturing and packaging facilities in the U.S., bringing their total planned investment to $265 billion. This move aims to strengthen the U.S. semiconductor ecosystem.
